message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Introduction</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The US has continued to see excess mortality through the Delta and Omicron periods. We sought to quantify excess mortality on a state level and calculate potential deaths averted if all states matched the excess mortality rates of those with the 10 lowest excess mortality rates.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Observational cohort, US and state-level data. Expected monthly deaths were modeled using pre-pandemic US and state-level data (2015-2020). Mortality data was accessed from CDC public reporting.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We find that during the Delta and Omicron waves, the US recorded over 596,000 excess deaths. 60% of the nation’s total excess mortality during these periods could have been averted if all states had excess mortality rates equal to those with the 10 lowest excess mortality rates.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>With large differences in excess mortality across US states in this 15-month study period, we note that a significant portion of deaths could have been averted with higher vaccination rates, improved mitigation policies, and adherence to other behaviors.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: State-Level Excess Mortality in US Adults During the Delta and Omicron Waves of COVID-19

reference.unstructured: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. Provisional Mortality Statistics, CDC WONDER. Accessed December 6, 2022. https://wonder.cdc.gov/mcd-icd10.html
Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. COVID Data Tracker. Published March 28, 2020. Accessed December 6, 2022. https://covid.cdc.gov/covid-data-tracker
